description_long: This is a rectangular barn with a classic gable roof covered in overlapping, weathered shingles. The front and rear gable ends are clad in vertical wooden planks, showing signs of age and wear, while the long sides are finished with a rough, patchy surface that appears to be either aged wood or corrugated metal. The barn features a large, central sliding door on the front gable, reinforced with visible cross-bracing and metal hardware. There are no visible windows on any side, giving the structure a utilitarian, enclosed appearance. The overall look is rustic and functional, typical of agricultural outbuildings found on the outskirts of urban or suburban environments.
description: A weathered, rectangular barn with a gable shingle roof, vertical plank front, and large sliding door. The sides are rough and patchy, with no visible windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the gable end with a large, central sliding door and vertical plank siding.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long, rough-textured wall and the full expanse of the shingled gable roof.
description_view_2: Rear gable view with no visible door or windows, featuring a mix of vertical planks and patchy siding.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a similarly rough, patchy wall and the shingled roof visible from above.
attribute_siding_pattern: vertical board siding on gable ends, rough patchy cladding on long sides
attribute_door_style: single large sliding door with visible cross-bracing
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows on any side
